jokes.text: I don't have to make a wish, because I already have this wonderful family. I wish for infinity hamburgers.
jokes.setup: Homer says he doesn't need to make a wish because he has everything he needs
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself with a ridiculous food-based wish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - the quick pivot from sentiment to gluttony is expertly timed and perfectly in character

jokes.text: Oh, I get it. These are those trick candles you can't blow out. No, Dad, they're the opposite. 'E-Z BLO.' This ends now.
jokes.setup: Homer assumes the candles won't blow out when they actually blow out too easily
jokes.punchline: The dramatic overreaction to easy-blow candles combined with the absurd product name
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy setup with good product parody, though fairly straightforward

jokes.text: It's okay, Homie. You're probably just tired from not doing anything all day.
jokes.setup: Marge comforting Homer after his candle failure
jokes.punchline: The logical impossibility of being tired from doing nothing, delivered as sincere comfort
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge line - supportive tone masking a burn, very quotable

jokes.text: Dad, you're out of shape even for an American.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Homer's fitness level
jokes.punchline: The qualifier 'even for an American' adds a layer of cultural satire
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural satire packed into a concise insult, very quotable

jokes.text: How many did I get? None? That's cool.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about his exercise performance
jokes.punchline: The casual acceptance of complete failure
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's obliviousness and lack of ambition

jokes.text: You know how many fires are started by birthday candles? If you do, tell me. It would settle a bet down at the station house. I say five, Gus says a million.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum asking about fire statistics
jokes.punchline: The absurdly wide gap between 5 and a million, showing complete ignorance of reasonable estimates
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum moment - the absurd bet parameters show his complete disconnect from reality

jokes.text: Feels good to tell the truth. No, I'm lying again, it feels bad.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming it feels good to tell the truth
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction revealing he's lying about lying feeling good
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about the nature of confession and dishonesty, good structure

jokes.text: Our kids used to be so cute. Used to?! Oh, deal with it.
jokes.setup: Marge making innocent comment about childhood photos
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal of Lisa's hurt feelings with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Marge's uncharacteristically harsh dismissal

jokes.text: So I made this elaborate decision tree, which in itself is worthy of preservation.
jokes.setup: Lisa explaining her methodical approach to choosing one item for the safe
jokes.punchline: The recursive logic problem of the decision-making tool becoming the thing worth saving
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment showing her overthinking creating its own problem, very smart writing

jokes.text: I picked my Malibu Stacy hybrid convertible. It runs on her old make up and out-of-style shoes.
jokes.setup: Lisa choosing a toy for the safe
jokes.punchline: The environmentally conscious but impractical fuel source for the toy car
jokes.explanation: Good blend of Lisa's environmental consciousness with absurd toy logic

jokes.text: Where do the kids today get these band names? 'The Kinks?' 'The Stones?' Sounds like my last physical!
jokes.setup: Grandpa complaining about modern band names, citing classic rock bands
jokes.punchline: Relating the names to his medical ailments, plus the irony that these aren't modern bands
jokes.explanation: Classic Grandpa confusion joke with multiple layers - generational disconnect and medical humor

jokes.text: You see all those crazy new electronics? iPod... iTunes... I give up!
jokes.setup: Grandpa overwhelmed by modern technology
jokes.punchline: Immediate surrender after naming just two related Apple products
jokes.explanation: Standard old person/technology joke, elevated by Grandpa's immediate capitulation

jokes.text: I don't know-- maybe the Krusty doll accidentally turned on the car's headlight, which focused on the cologne bottle, causing it to boil, and soon explode.
jokes.setup: Trying to explain what caused the safe fire
jokes.punchline: Overly elaborate Rube Goldberg-style explanation for simple cause-and-effect
jokes.explanation: Lisa's scientific mind creating an unnecessarily complex explanation, though a bit verbose

jokes.text: What's the deal with this 'California pizza'? If I wanted... cheese and fruit, I'd have to...
jokes.setup: Homer doing a Seinfeld-style 'What's the deal' setup about California pizza
jokes.punchline: Gets interrupted before completing the thought, subverting the expected punchline
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke referencing Seinfeld's style but interrupted before payoff, more clever than funny

jokes.text: It's like what a resume is for a man.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining the importance of the family album
jokes.punchline: Comparing family photos to male professional achievement, showing different value systems
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's traditional values and gender role awareness

jokes.text: Lisa, you fool. You've doomed us all.
jokes.setup: Lisa suggesting they restage family photos
jokes.punchline: Bart's dramatic overreaction treating photo recreation as an apocalyptic mistake
jokes.explanation: Good Bart reaction with appropriate melodrama for the situation

jokes.text: Oh, man, I thought I was strong enough to get through this again, but I'm not.
jokes.setup: Recreating a Star Trek: Voyager finale party
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's emotional breakdown over a TV show ending, treating it like genuine trauma
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - his over-investment in fictional media treated as real grief

jokes.text: Oh, Captain Janeway! Your mission ended too soon. Too soon.
jokes.setup: Continuing his emotional breakdown over Star Trek
jokes.punchline: The repetition of 'too soon' adds to the melodrama of grieving a TV show
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of the previous joke, the repetition adds to the absurdity

jokes.text: Here I am on the space shuttle, orbiting Earth. It sure was nice of NASA to send you up again.
jokes.setup: Looking at restaged family photos
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to Homer apparently being a repeat astronaut, as if it's normal
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Homer's previous space adventures, treated matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Duffman's on a date with Booberella. He's supposed to be in a stable, long-term gay relationship.
jokes.setup: Spotting celebrities in background of photo
jokes.punchline: Marge's casual knowledge of celebrity gossip and sexual orientation expectations
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between Marge's innocent demeanor and detailed celebrity gossip knowledge

jokes.text: Like Lindsay Lohan looking drunk, high and bony. Click, click, career over, see ya in the gutter, freckles!
jokes.setup: Editor explaining what makes a good celebrity scandal photo
jokes.punchline: The callous celebration of a young celebrity's downfall, ending with 'freckles' as a final insult
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of tabloid culture, 'freckles' as final insult is oddly memorable

jokes.text: Moderately wealthy? Whee! I'm sort of rich! I can rent anything I want!
jokes.setup: Editor offering to make Homer moderately wealthy
jokes.punchline: Homer's excitement over modest success and his interpretation of wealth as rental access
jokes.explanation: Good Homer character moment showing his low expectations and misunderstanding of wealth

jokes.text: Your head dinged my door panel! Jerks like you ought to be shot!
jokes.setup: Krusty getting out of his car at a premiere
jokes.punchline: Extreme overreaction to minor property damage, inappropriate for a children's entertainer
jokes.explanation: Classic Krusty behavior - violent outburst contrasting with his public persona

jokes.text: How about you, Gabbo? Ay-yi-yi-yi-yi-yi-yi! Okay, how about an autograph for my kids? Well, when you put it that way... Ay-yi-yi-yi-yi yi-yi-yi-yi!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get Gabbo to cooperate for photos and autographs
jokes.punchline: Gabbo can only respond with his catchphrase, showing his one-dimensional nature
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established character, though the joke is somewhat repetitive

jokes.text: Imported beer? Seedless grapes? Chewing gum? The clown on this cereal box is just a person.
jokes.setup: Homer amazed by upscale grocery store products
jokes.punchline: The progression from fancy products to the ultimate luxury - realistic cereal mascots
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with the final item being the most absurd 'luxury' - very quotable and original

jokes.text: Hey, Paris, I saw a disgusting part of your body on the Internet-- your face! I'm still breaking in this nose!
jokes.setup: Bart appearing to reference inappropriate internet content
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by insulting her face, followed by her defensive response about plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with effective setup/payoff, plus the follow-up about plastic surgery

jokes.text: Hello, Homer. Have you lost weight? Oh, like you care, Betty White. Tell me, how's Maggie? Her name is Marge! I was talking about your baby.
jokes.setup: Betty White being genuinely friendly to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's paranoid response followed by confusion about family member names
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's suspicious nature even when faced with genuine kindness

jokes.text: Your hip-hop CD was boastful and unnecessary.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to provoke Drederick Tatum
jokes.punchline: The polite, measured criticism of hip-hop delivered as if it's fighting words
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between Homer's mild critique and the expectation of inflammatory insults, very quotable

jokes.text: What are the first three? Nobility, clergy and commoners. Learn your French history.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about the 'first three estates' after Tatum mentions the fourth estate
jokes.punchline: The boxer giving a knowledgeable history lesson, subverting expectations about his intelligence
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of boxer stereotype - Tatum being more educated than expected

jokes.text: What the hell did you do that for? I wanted to remember my place in the book I was reading.
jokes.setup: Homer questioning why Tatum hit him
jokes.punchline: The completely benign and intellectual reason for the violence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - violence for the most civilized reason possible, continues the Tatum intelligence theme

jokes.text: Boy, I never thought I'd have my own darkroom. Yes, but why does it have to be in my bedroom?
jokes.setup: Homer excited about having a darkroom
jokes.punchline: Lisa's discomfort about the inappropriate location choice
jokes.explanation: Basic setup about inappropriate room usage, gets the job done but not particularly creative

jokes.text: Kids they always say they're not tired when they're the most tired of all.
jokes.setup: Lisa falling asleep while helping with photo development
jokes.punchline: Homer's parental wisdom delivered while completely missing that she's passed out from chemical fumes
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness - treating chemical-induced unconsciousness as normal childhood behavior

jokes.text: Sideshow Mel is in a custody battle?! And it's turning bitter? If you read one more thing, it is a purchase.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to resist reading the magazine
jokes.punchline: Her immediate fascination with celebrity gossip followed by the store policy threat
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the previous setup, showing Marge's hypocrisy about tabloid consumption

jokes.text: Did I attach the harness?! No! Should attach it for you?! If you don't mind...!
jokes.setup: Homer preparing for aerial photography
jokes.punchline: The casual discovery of a life-threatening safety oversight mid-flight
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup with the polite tone contrasting with the danger

jokes.text: Do you, Rainier Luftwaffe Wolfcastle, take Maria Shriver-Kennedy-Quimby... Up, up, and annoy!
jokes.setup: Wedding ceremony being interrupted by Homer's aerial photography
jokes.punchline: Homer's Superman parody catchphrase modified for his paparazzi mission
jokes.explanation: Clever Superman reference modification, plus the absurdly long hyphenated bride name

jokes.text: Yes! I also wreck bar mitzvahs!
jokes.setup: Homer successfully disrupting the wedding
jokes.punchline: His proud declaration of ruining other religious ceremonies
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's expanding disruption of sacred events

jokes.text: First Amendment rules, privacy drools!
jokes.setup: Homer justifying his paparazzi behavior
jokes.punchline: Childish rhyming slogan about constitutional rights
jokes.explanation: Very quotable and economical, perfect Homer-level understanding of constitutional law

jokes.text: Krusty, your response was the least funny. I'm a visual comedian. Did you get the j-peg? Wouldn't open.
jokes.setup: Discussion of responses to an e-vite meeting
jokes.punchline: Krusty's excuse about being visual followed by technical incompetence
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Krusty's outdated technical skills and defensive nature

jokes.text: How are you gonna help us, Beefaroni?
jokes.setup: Introduction of Italian photographer Enrico Irritazio
jokes.punchline: Krusty's mispronunciation/mockery as a pasta brand name
jokes.explanation: Standard ethnic name humor, elevated by Krusty's dismissive delivery

jokes.text: Now please, take some sushi, 'cause if I give it to the dogs, they will think, 'Oh, now I get sushi all the time.' And I am not made of sushi.
jokes.setup: Rainier offering sushi to meeting attendees
jokes.punchline: The elaborate logic about pet expectations and the literal disclaimer about not being made of sushi
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic combined with the literal 'not made of sushi' declaration, very quotable

jokes.text: Now, Maggie, I had to use your car seat to hold the new TV Guide... so I'm gonna hang you from the mirror.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining a logistics problem to his baby
jokes.punchline: The casual child endangerment presented as a reasonable solution
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about child safety delivered with Homer's characteristic oblivious tone

jokes.text: He's trying to make me look like a bad father. Maggie, take the wheel! Ten and two, Einstein!
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about being portrayed as irresponsible
jokes.punchline: Immediately proving the point by having his baby drive while giving driving instruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic timing - denying bad parenting while demonstrating it, plus the Einstein reference to a baby

jokes.text: I have no friends. Friends take time. And time is money, and money pays for things, like a manicure and a boiled egg.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about their friendship
jokes.punchline: The cold business logic ending with mundane luxury items
jokes.explanation: Good character development for the editor, showing his mercenary nature and modest aspirations

jokes.text: So long, anti-clotting agent! Clot, clot, clot, clot clot, clot, clot, clot!
jokes.setup: Homer giving up his medicine along with photography
jokes.punchline: The enthusiastic embrace of medical danger with rhythmic chanting
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with Homer's characteristic all-or-nothing approach to life changes

jokes.text: With Homer Simpson out of the way, we can go back to our lives of sybaritic excess. Stem cell fajita?
jokes.setup: Celebrities celebrating Homer's retirement
jokes.punchline: Using sophisticated vocabulary followed by absurd futuristic food
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between highbrow vocabulary and absurd sci-fi cuisine, quotable

jokes.text: You know who I can't stand? That Robin Williams. You know, one time I saw him eating dinner with his children. He wouldn't take the time out to do all the funny bits from his movies.
jokes.setup: Barney expressing celebrity hatred
jokes.punchline: The unreasonable expectation that celebrities perform their act during private family time
jokes.explanation: Great satire of fan entitlement and celebrity expectations, shows character ignorance

jokes.text: And my sister once saw Burt Reynolds at an airport, and he wouldn't even cosign her mortgage.
jokes.setup: Continuing the theme of unreasonable celebrity expectations
jokes.punchline: The completely inappropriate request for financial assistance from a stranger
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of the previous joke, the mortgage cosigning is wonderfully absurd and specific

jokes.text: I was gonna use it to take secret photos in the ladies' toilet, but, uh, no dames ever come in this joint.
jokes.setup: Moe offering Homer a camera
jokes.punchline: The admission of planned voyeurism followed by the sad reality of his bar's unpopularity
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe line combining creepy behavior with self-deprecating business reality

jokes.text: Excuse me, do you have a ladies' room? We need to trade bras and panties. Oh, you got to be kidding me!
jokes.setup: Moe just lamenting no women come to his bar
jokes.punchline: Immediate arrival of women with an inappropriate request, proving his camera plan was wrong anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect immediate callback with ironic timing, plus the absurd underwear trading request

jokes.text: You call this a drink? No, I never called it a drink.
jokes.setup: Customer complaining about the quality of their beverage
jokes.punchline: Moe's literal defense avoiding responsibility through technicality
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moe line showing his literal-minded business practices and poor service

jokes.text: Let one of us regular guys write a terrible children's book!
jokes.setup: Homer making demands of celebrities for better behavior
jokes.punchline: The specific and self-aware request for literary mediocrity opportunities
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of celebrity vanity publishing and regular people's modest aspirations, very quotable

jokes.text: Say, Rainier, you're in show business. Would you mind taking a look at this screenplay I wrote? Oh, sorry. I do not read unsolicited manuscripts.
jokes.setup: Homer asking for help with his screenplay at a friendly barbecue
jokes.punchline: Rainier's immediate return to Hollywood business practices despite the social setting
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing celebrities can't escape their business personas even in friendly settings

jokes.text: Well, at least it got made.
jokes.setup: Homer's screenplay rejection
jokes.punchline: Homer's optimistic interpretation while presumably looking at something terrible
jokes.explanation: Homer's eternal optimism even in failure, likely with visual gag
